ai_strategy_lead.name: Song Fu
ai_strategy_lead.title: Director, Applied AI and Data Science Institute

documents.title: UNT Applied Artificial Intelligence & Data Science Institute mission and focus areas
UNT System Generative AI site (Guiding Principles, approved tools, FAQ)
UNT System Guiding Principles for Generative AI
R1 Our Way Task Force strategic framework

ai_strategy.summary: UNT operates a two-layer strategy: the UNT System maintains a public 'Generative AI at UNT System' hub that centralizes guiding principles, approved enterprise tools (Copilot, M365 Copilot, UNiTyAI), and training; the Denton campus hosts the Applied AI and Data Science Institute, which frames AI as a core engine for research, workforce training, and industry partnerships. The strategy emphasizes 'inclusive and responsible AI,' ties directly to the R1 Our Way tier-one mission, and treats AI as a cross-college capability spanning engineering, information, business, science, and health.

key_provisions: Syllabus-level AI policy is required on every course syllabus
Unauthorized AI use is treated as cheating under Student Academic Integrity Policy 06.003
Detection reports (e.g., Turnitin) alone are insufficient evidence of violations
Users must not input sensitive, confidential, or personal data into public AI tools
FERPA and HIPAA compliance explicitly required for AI-assisted workflows
AI-generated outputs must be verified, attributed, and disclosed
System-approved stack: Copilot Chat (public data), Microsoft 365 Copilot (internal), UNiTyAI (secure Azure-native)
ai_policy.summary: UNT couples faculty discretion at the syllabus level with a strong System-level policy floor. The UNT System 'Guiding Principles for Generative AI' spell out privacy, accountability, ethics, and limitations, and direct users to institution-approved tools. Academic Integrity Policy 06.003 treats unauthorized AI use as cheating, while faculty must publish explicit syllabus language on permitted AI use.

bodies.scope: University-wide hub for AI research, education, workforce training, and industry partnerships

bodies.name: UNT System Generative AI program (ai.untsystem.edu)

bodies.name: Academic Integrity Task Force
bodies.scope: Faculty Senate task force addressing AI-related academic integrity, syllabus guidance, and support for faculty, administrators, and students

bodies.name: R1 Our Way Task Force
bodies.scope: Strategic initiative elevating UNT's R1 research posture; sets the context in which AI is funded and organized across colleges

governance_structure.summary: Governance is split: the UNT System runs centralized generative-AI governance (approved tools, principles, FERPA/HIPAA guardrails), while the Denton campus governs academic AI through the Applied AI and Data Science Institute and a Faculty Senate–linked Academic Integrity Task Force. UNT has not publicly designated a Chief AI Officer.

initiatives.title: UNT launches Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence (Fall 2026)
initiatives.description: UNT's Board of Regents approved a 120-hour Bachelor of Science in AI, housed in the College of Engineering at Discovery Park, with a July/Fall 2026 launch. Curriculum spans applied AI, software development for AI, machine learning, autonomous systems, and interdisciplinary offerings such as 'AI in Games,' with an embedded focus on ethics and societal implications.
initiatives.significance: Makes UNT one of the first public universities in Texas to offer a standalone undergraduate AI degree, aimed at the 118,000+ AI-adjacent job openings in the DFW market.

investment.funding_source: UNT Board of Regents / state allocation
investment.description: Board-approved new degree with projected median first-year salary of $68,208 and five-year median of $105,335; cost of degree estimated at $34,000.

collaborations.description: Program rationale built on DFW labor-market demand signals.

initiatives.policy_implications: Formal recognition of AI as a discrete degree track signals institutional commitment to AI-native curricula and workforce alignment.

initiatives.title: UNT System UNiTyAI secure generative AI platform
initiatives.description: UNiTyAI is a private, Microsoft Azure-native generative AI platform offered System-wide (UNT, UNT HSC, UNT Dallas). It supports natural-language conversations, document/image analysis, and no-code custom GPTs for use cases such as admissions assistants, academic advising bots, IT helpdesk triage, and policy Q&A agents.
initiatives.significance: Gives the UNT System a sanctioned alternative to public chatbots, enabling FERPA/HIPAA-compliant AI deployment across academic and medical contexts.

investment.description: Enterprise platform built on Microsoft Azure; no public cost disclosure.

collaborations.description: Azure-hosted GenAI with Microsoft foundation models.

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a sanctioned data-safe AI channel, reducing shadow-IT use of consumer AI with student/patient data.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot Chat and Microsoft 365 Copilot rollout
initiatives.description: The UNT System provides Copilot Chat at no cost to all students, faculty, and staff for public-data use, and offers Microsoft 365 Copilot as a $20/user/month add-on license for faculty and staff across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive.
initiatives.significance: Standardizes the enterprise productivity AI stack across a 47,000+ student institution and its System partners, with explicit data-handling guardrails.

investment.description: Copilot Chat bundled at no cost; M365 Copilot as optional paid add-on.

collaborations.description: Enterprise Microsoft agreement extended to AI features.

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Free generative AI chat for all users

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Paid productivity AI across Office apps

initiatives.policy_implications: Users are directed away from public, sensitive-data inputs toward Copilot Chat's public-data mode and M365 Copilot's internal-data mode.

initiatives.title: NSF S-STEM AI Scholars program ($2M)
initiatives.description: UNT's Department of Computer Science and Engineering won a nearly $2 million NSF S-STEM grant to fund the 'AI Scholars' program. The award supports at least 10 scholarship students per cohort (renewable up to three years) through mentorship, specialized coursework, an AI certificate, and paid research in computer vision, bioinformatics, and cybersecurity.
initiatives.significance: Opens AI pathways to financially-qualifying undergraduates at an R1 public university and builds a mentor-driven pipeline for the DFW tech economy.

investment.description: Nearly $2M for scholarships, mentoring, and research experiences; PI Xiaohui Yuan with co-PIs Xuan Guo, David Keathly, Amar Maharjan, Kirill Morozov, and Gergely Záruba.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI Lab (launched August 2022)
initiatives.description: Directed by Dr. Yunhe Feng, UNT's Responsible AI Lab focuses on Efficient GenAI (long-context inference, RAG, edge AI, quantum-AI), Trustworthy AI (privacy, adversarial auditing, bias mitigation, explainability), and Applied Impact (biomedicine, education, agriculture, large-scale scientific modeling). Funders include NSF, Oak Ridge National Lab, Microsoft, Google, and NVIDIA.
initiatives.significance: Anchors UNT's public-facing responsible AI research agenda and gives the university a brand in trustworthy/efficient generative AI.

investment.description: Mixed federal and industry funding; specific totals not disclosed on lab page.

initiatives.policy_implications: Publishes research on adversarial auditing and bias mitigation that informs broader responsible AI practice.

initiatives.title: Sinha Innovation Lab for Data Science and Department of Data Science
initiatives.description: In 2024, Broadcom VP and UNT Ph.D. alum Vikas Sinha and his wife Anuradha endowed the Anuradha and Vikas Sinha Department of Data Science with $3M. In June 2025 they added $2.15M to launch the Sinha Innovation Lab for Data Science in UNT's College of Information (combined Sinha gifts exceed $5M), supporting student hands-on training, faculty research, and industry projects.
initiatives.significance: Privately funded data-science infrastructure in the College of Information strengthens UNT's applied AI pipeline beyond engineering.

investment.description: Combined Sinha gifts exceed $5M: $3M (2024) endowment for a named department; $2.15M (2025) for the innovation lab.

collaborations.description: Alumni/industry-driven funding with industry project pipeline.

initiatives.title: MOSAIC AI forensics tool ($2M NIJ grant)
initiatives.description: UNT Health Fort Worth won a $2M National Institute of Justice grant to build MOSAIC (Unifying Methods of Sex, Stature, Affinity & Age for Identification through Computational Standardization), an AI platform that standardizes the generation of biological profiles from skeletal remains to accelerate missing-persons identification. Once complete, the tool will be free to law enforcement and forensic anthropologists.
initiatives.significance: Real-world applied AI with criminal-justice impact, and a signature example of UNT's health-science AI research.

investment.description: $2M federal NIJ grant to UNT Health Fort Worth.

initiatives.title: Texas Talent Accelerator regional workforce initiative
initiatives.description: Launched in September 2025, the Texas Talent Accelerator is a regional initiative convened by UNT with colleges, employers, nonprofits, and economic development agencies. It aims to align credentials, programs, and curricula — including AI and data-science skills — with North Texas employer demand.
initiatives.significance: Positions UNT as a convening workforce-intermediary in the DFW AI economy and connects academic programs to employer demand signals.

investment.description: Regional coalition with multiple partners; specific funding not disclosed.

initiatives.title: Graduate Academic Certificate in Artificial Intelligence (online)
initiatives.description: UNT's College of Engineering offers an online Graduate Academic Certificate in AI covering core AI, software development for AI, and machine learning, structured as a stackable credential for working professionals.
initiatives.significance: Provides an adult-learner on-ramp into AI competency and feeds UNT's MS in AI pipeline.

initiatives.title: AI in Professional Communication undergraduate certificate
initiatives.description: A four-course undergraduate certificate that teaches students to leverage AI tools, navigate ethical challenges, and produce data-informed content for AI-augmented workplaces.
initiatives.significance: Extends AI skills beyond engineering into communication and liberal-arts disciplines — a signal of cross-college AI literacy.

initiatives.title: AI4ALL College Pathways partnership
initiatives.description: UNT is one of the first four universities paired with national nonprofit AI4ALL to offer free AI summer experiences for rising high-school students, with explicit focus on underrepresented groups. Programming covers machine learning fundamentals, neural networks, NLP, AI ethics, and project work with faculty mentors.
initiatives.significance: Concrete K-16 equity pipeline for AI talent that broadens who enters the AI workforce.

investment.description: Pre-college summer programming with free-to-student enrollment.

initiatives.title: UNT Dallas campus-wide AI integration and AI Community of Practice (2024-25)
initiatives.description: During AY 2024-25, UNT Dallas laid the foundation for campus-wide AI adoption: an AI Community of Practice was launched, 138 faculty/staff (67% of faculty) engaged in AI learning, and training from external experts (e.g., Dr. Jose Bowen, co-author of Teaching with AI) was delivered via workshops, webinars, and micro-credentials.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates faculty-development scale across the UNT System and positions UNT Dallas as a peer sibling in AI pedagogy.

partners: AAC&U Institute on AI, Pedagogy, and the Curriculum (2025-26)

initiatives.title: Academic Integrity Task Force on AI and syllabus-level policy
initiatives.description: UNT's Academic Integrity Office, working with the Faculty Senate, convened a task force of faculty (tenured, tenure-track, and non-tenure-track), administrators, and student representatives to address AI concerns across campus. Outputs include required syllabus language on AI, FAQ resources for faculty, and guidance that AI detector reports alone are insufficient evidence for a violation.
initiatives.significance: Institutionalizes the handling of AI within academic integrity infrastructure and pushes policy decisions into every course.

investment.description: Faculty Senate task force; no dedicated line-item disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets the operational model for AI-in-teaching governance at the instructor level.

initiatives.title: Talon3 HPC cluster and UNT Research Computing
initiatives.description: UNT's main HPC facility, Talon3, is a 350-node heterogeneous Dell PowerEdge cluster (including NVIDIA Tesla K80 GPUs) managed with Bright Cluster Manager and operated by the North Texas Scientific Computing unit, supporting AI, materials science, engineering, chemistry, and physics workloads.
initiatives.significance: Provides the campus-level compute substrate for AI and deep-learning research across multiple colleges.

investment.description: Institutional HPC with federal and state grant-funded node additions.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Talon3 is UNT's 350-node Dell PowerEdge HPC cluster, including NVIDIA Tesla K80 GPU nodes, managed by Bright Cluster Manager and operated by North Texas Scientific Computing. It supports AI, ML, and scientific computing across engineering, materials science, chemistry, physics, and informatics. Specific current GPU counts are not published on the public site.

regulatory_context.summary: Texas passed the Responsible AI Governance Act (HB 149 / 'Texas RAIGA') creating a statewide regulatory framework, a Texas AI Council within the Department of Information Resources, and a sandbox program — effective January 1, 2026. A December 11, 2025 federal executive order seeks to preempt state AI laws, creating enforcement uncertainty for Texas public universities. The UNT System maintains its own AI policy layer (guiding principles, approved tools, FERPA/HIPAA guardrails) and is not in the University of Texas System.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: Detection tools alone (e.g., Turnitin) are formally declared insufficient evidence for AI-related academic-integrity violations at UNT — a policy response to faculty over-reliance on AI detectors.

Under Texas SB 17 (anti-DEI law), UNT eliminated its DEI department; this indirectly affects how UNT frames AI equity and underrepresented-student initiatives, which must now be positioned outside a DEI umbrella.

narrative_summary: The University of North Texas presents a mid-maturity, dual-layer AI posture that is accelerating rapidly. At the System level, UNT operates a centralized generative-AI program with published guiding principles, a three-tier approved stack (Copilot Chat at no cost, Microsoft 365 Copilot as a paid add-on, and the Azure-native UNiTyAI for data-sensitive workloads), and formal FERPA/HIPAA guardrails. At the Denton campus, the Applied AI and Data Science Institute, led by Director Song Fu with three associate directors across engineering, data science, and analytics, serves as the university-wide hub linking 30+ AI faculty, seven named research labs (including the Responsible AI Lab founded in 2022 and funded by NSF, ORNL, Microsoft, Google, and NVIDIA), and a Talon3 HPC cluster. On the academic side, UNT runs one of Texas's earliest MS in AI programs (2020) and has received Board of Regents approval for a Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence launching in July/Fall 2026, along with a set of online and undergraduate certificates (including one in AI for Professional Communication). The pipeline is reinforced by an NSF S-STEM $2M 'AI Scholars' grant, the AI4ALL partnership for underrepresented high-school students, and a $5M+ Sinha family gift that established a named Department of Data Science (2024) and Sinha Innovation Lab (2025). UNT Health Fort Worth anchors applied-science AI with a $2M NIJ grant for MOSAIC, an AI forensic tool for missing-persons identification. Governance is split among the System GenAI program, the AI Institute, and a Faculty Senate Academic Integrity Task Force that requires every syllabus to publish AI policy. UNT has not designated a Chief AI Officer. The bigger contextual constraints are Texas SB 17 (forcing AI-equity work outside a DEI frame) and the incoming Texas RAIGA regime plus a December 2025 federal preemption executive order. Gaps include no publicly quantified GPU counts, no published campus-wide enterprise AI adoption metrics outside UNT Dallas's faculty-training numbers, and no single consolidated UNT-Denton AI strategy document comparable to peer R1s.

gaps_acknowledged: No publicly designated Chief AI Officer at UNT.
No single consolidated UNT-Denton strategic AI plan document comparable to peer R1 strategies.
Exact GPU counts and current scale of Talon3 not disclosed on the public HPC site.
UNiTyAI deployment metrics (user counts, pilot dates) not publicly disclosed.
Launch dates for several academic certificates (Undergraduate AI Certificate; AI in Professional Communication) not published.
No public reporting of syllabus-level adherence to AI policy across the full course catalog.
Student mental-health-and-wellbeing AI deployments and LMS-embedded AI (e.g., Canvas integrations) were not surfaced in public sources.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
